results.title: Deng's legacy: China as an economic force
results.creator_credit: Alex Gibney, Richard Hall and New Eagle International
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “China: unleashing the dragon”.
China’s emergence as a global economic force and dramatic changes within Chinese life are due largely to the policies instituted under Deng Xiaoping. This program examines Deng’s career and the continuing impact of his economic policies. The old style of communism, with its policy of isolation from the West and self-sufficiency, is evolving into a socialist market economy. Joint ventures and foreign investment are becoming popular. The program profiles several such projects and also looks at “special economic zones” such as Hainan which have greater autonomy from the central government. Also looks at some of the problems emerging as a result of rapid change and economic growth, such as prostitution, rural poverty, untempered greed and corruption. Narrated by Joan Chen.

results.title: The Fragile rice bowl: workers in New China
results.creator_credit: Brenda Louie, Richard Alexander and New Eagle International
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “China: unleashing the dragon”.
In Communist China, a worker had a guiding concept in life. It was known as the “iron rice bowl” or the notion that one would always be fed. In a brave new China, this concept has been shattered. Profiles Jessica - from her now abandoned job at the state-run factory to the new world of selling cosmetics. Shows the impact of her decision on her own life and the life of her family.

results.title: The Mediterranean
results.creator_credit: Bertand Morin, Christopher Bryson, Joe Wiecha, Marianna Edmunds, Tom Naughton, Nicolas Valcour, Learning Channel, New Dominion Pictures and Quai 32
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Archaeology”.
John Rhys-Davis narrates five excellent episodes which showcases archaeological exploration around the Mediterranean. French divers discover a cave filled with 18,000 year old rock paintings in “A Cave beneath the sea.” The Minoan colony of the island of Thera is shown in “Lost city of the Aegean.” The daily life of ancient Egypt is unlocked in “Forgotten mummies.” After World War 2, Schliemann’s discoveries at Troy, disappeared and the mystery is investigated in “Lost treasures of Troy.” The secret rituals of the women of Lesbos in Ancient Greece is the subject of the final episode entitled “Women of Lesbos.”
results.creator_credit_for_label: Bertand Morin, Christopher Bryson, Joe Wiecha, Marianna Edmunds, Tom Naughton, Nicolas Valcour, Learning Channel, New Dominion Pictures and Quai 32

results.title: Gandhi
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Twentieth century”.
Traces Gandhi’s rise to power in India and his struggles to win independence for his country, terminating in his assassination on January 30, 1948. Narrated by Walter Cronkite.

results.title: Lenin and Trotsky
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Twentieth century”.
Shows how Lenin and Trotsky made possible the seizure of the Russian Government and the consolidation of Communist power in the USSR, and how Lenin became a Communist hero, while Trotsky became the arch-enemy. Suitable for upper secondary level.
